title: Should You Sell Your Home Before Things Get Worse?

h2_sections.text: What is actually happening to Canadian homeowners right now?
h2_sections.filename: AskRoss.ca - 1 - What is actually happening to Canadian homeowners right now.png
h2_sections.prompt_hint: A montage of a declining home-price chart, a worried Canadian family, and a stack of renewal notices on a kitchen counter, muted newsprint colour palette.

h2_sections.text: How are mortgage renewals making this worse?
h2_sections.filename: AskRoss.ca - 2 - How are mortgage renewals making this worse.png
h2_sections.prompt_hint: A kitchen table covered in stacked bills and a calendar with a renewal date circled in red, a Canadian couple looking worried, overhead lighting casting shadows.

h2_sections.text: What happens when your equity disappears?
h2_sections.filename: AskRoss.ca - 3 - What happens when your equity disappears.png
h2_sections.prompt_hint: A model house sinking into a pile of sand, an empty piggy bank beside it, muted tones suggesting financial loss, neutral studio background.

h2_sections.text: What are the warning signs that you're heading toward a forced sale?
h2_sections.filename: AskRoss.ca - 4 - What are the warning signs that you're heading toward a forced sale.png
h2_sections.prompt_hint: A visibly stressed Canadian homeowner holding an overdue mortgage notice, red warning flags visible in the background, serious and urgent expression.

h2_sections.text: Why is waiting for the market to recover a risky strategy?
h2_sections.filename: AskRoss.ca - 5 - Why is waiting for the market to recover a risky strategy.png
h2_sections.prompt_hint: A calendar with months being crossed off while a home-price chart stays flat, a Canadian homeowner sitting on a couch looking at phone bills accumulating on the coffee table.

h2_sections.text: How does selling your home actually affect your credit?
h2_sections.filename: AskRoss.ca - 6 - How does selling your home actually affect your credit.png
h2_sections.prompt_hint: A clean credit score meter jumping from the red zone to green after a successful home sale, a Canadian homeowner's relieved face reflected in a laptop screen.

h2_sections.text: What does a financial reset actually look like?
h2_sections.filename: AskRoss.ca - 7 - What does a financial reset actually look like.png
h2_sections.prompt_hint: A clean modern desk with a fresh notepad, a sunrise through a window, a Canadian couple reviewing a new budget plan with an optimistic fresh-start mood.

h2_sections.text: Advice from Ross Taylor Mortgages: Knowing when it's time to sell your home
h2_sections.filename: AskRoss.ca - 8 - Advice from Ross Taylor Mortgages_ Knowing when it's time to sell your home.png
h2_sections.prompt_hint: A friendly Canadian mortgage advisor speaking directly to the camera in a modern Toronto office, notepad and laptop open, warm professional lighting.

h2_sections.text: List of all FAQs: Canadian homeowners who can't afford their renewal or refinance
h2_sections.filename: AskRoss.ca - 9 - List of all FAQs_ Canadian homeowners who can't afford their renewal or refinance.png
h2_sections.prompt_hint: A clean split-screen infographic showing common mortgage questions on one side and concise answers on the other, Canadian flag subtly visible in the background.
